Inferred responsibility: Test whether a dialog control ID is one of the known hyperlink controls.

Inferred responsibility: Return hyperlink display target or click target.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass procedure for hyperlink-like static controls.

Inferred responsibility: Initialize about-dialog hyperlink controls.
Evidence: Gets dialog items from link table, creates underlined font, sizes controls, subclasses each with `FUN_00401133`.

Inferred responsibility: Owner-draw the hyperlink controls.
Evidence: Selects visited/unvisited colors, draws URL/mail text with `ExtTextOutA`.

Inferred responsibility: Open a hyperlink or email address with the shell and mark it visited.
Evidence: Uses `FUN_004010ba(..., 1)` to get `mailto:` or web URL, calls `ShellExecuteA(..., "open", ...)`, invalidates the link control.

Inferred responsibility: Release hyperlink font resource.
Evidence: Deletes cached font object.

Inferred responsibility: Application entry/main message loop.
Evidence: Registers main window plus `DiagOutWindow` and `DiagInWindow`, loads RichEdit DLL, creates main window, loads accelerators, runs `GetMessageA`/`TranslateMessage`/`DispatchMessageA`.

Inferred responsibility: Main window procedure and central command dispatcher.
Evidence: Registered as main class wndproc; handles many `WM_COMMAND` IDs, opens dialogs, launches map/minimize/import/export/save flows, calls `DefWindowProcA` fallback.

Inferred responsibility: MINTIME dialog procedure for configuring and returning minimize-time benchmark parameters. It initializes numeric spinner controls, radio/check boxes, enables/disables percent-true input, and on OK writes selected inputs/outputs/repetitions/percent mode/exact-fast/log flags back to the caller parameter block.
Evidence: Passed to DialogBoxParamA(..., "MINTIME", ..., FUN_00409747, &local_13f0) in FUN_004016d2. Handles WM_INITDIALOG and WM_COMMAND, initializes up-down controls 0x484/0x486/0x488/0x48a, reads edit controls 0x483/0x485/0x487/0x489, checks controls 0x408/0x42a/0x48b/0x48c, and caller uses the returned fields for MINIMIZE TIME TEST timing runs with FUN_0040c55b.

Inferred responsibility: Recreates or imports a stored gate diagram into the active diagram-entry UI by replaying gate/object placement and wire-connection actions.
Evidence: Starts diagram entry with SendMessageA(DAT_00452aac, WM_COMMAND, 0xcd, 0), iterates saved diagram objects at param_1 + 0x3a4 / count param_1 + 0x1650, maps saved object types to gate-palette command IDs 0x3f4..0x402, 0x430, 0x438, 0x439, sends mouse/command messages to DAT_00452a98, uses FUN_0040a1fb for Variable Name dialogs, then replays wire connections using stored pin coordinates and source indexes.

Inferred responsibility: Worker thread that auto-fills and accepts the modal Variable Name dialog during scripted diagram reconstruction/input-output node insertion.
Evidence: FUN_00409b22 sends command 0x438/0x439, then starts FUN_0040a1fb with __beginthread; this function waits for FindWindowA("#32770", "Variable Name"), writes dialog item 0x43e via SetDlgItemTextA, sends WM_COMMAND IDOK, then exits via FUN_0043ea5f. For command 0x439 it forces name "X"; otherwise it uses the supplied name pointer.

Inferred responsibility: Central UI error-message dispatcher: decodes a packed Logic Friday error code, formats the matching message, and shows it with MessageBoxA.
Evidence: Switches on param_2 >> 0x10, formats strings such as "Syntax error in equation.", "No function is selected.", "Unable to map the function.", and "File error %d.", then calls MessageBoxA(param_1, local_10c, caption, 0). Call sites pass packed constants like 0x40001, 0x50000, 0x1a0000, 0x2d0040, and 0x2f000a from startup, selection validation, minimization/mapping, parsing, file, and external-tool flows.

Inferred responsibility: Thin Win32 dialog-procedure wrapper for the TTSETUP truth-table setup dialog; initializes/caches the dialog context on WM_INITDIALOG, then delegates all message handling to FUN_00418362.
Evidence: Called as DialogBoxParamA(..., "TTSETUP", ..., FUN_0040ad29, 0x46bf70). Before the call, 0x46bf70 is populated with &DAT_0046c2d0 and &DAT_00452b30. On WM_INITDIALOG, FUN_0040ad29 stores lParam globally, then delegates to FUN_00418362, which initializes input/output count controls, validates variable names, writes labels/counts into the truth-table data block, allocates output truth-table arrays sized 1 << inputCount, and ends the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure thunk for the gate-mapping options dialog MAPDIALOG; binds the current function/model object, then forwards dialog messages to the object-specific handler that builds user.genlib from selected gate types.
Evidence: Main gate mapping command calls FUN_0040ad72(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac), then DialogBoxParamA(..., "MAPDIALOG", ..., FUN_0040ad72, 0). Message 0x8008 stores param_4 in DAT_0046c4d8; later messages call FUN_00422090(DAT_0046c4d8, hwnd, msg, command). FUN_00422090 handles WM_INITDIALOG/WM_COMMAND, checks NAND/NOR controls, validates required gate type selection, writes GATE nand*/nor* definitions to %s\\user.genlib, and ends the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: VARNAMEDLG dialog-procedure trampoline for adding a new gate-diagram input/output variable name.
Evidence: Called as DialogBoxParamA(..., "VARNAMEDLG", ..., FUN_0040adb4, gateType) when placing gate types 0x438 / 0x439. It saves param_4 into DAT_0046c4dc on message 0x8008; otherwise calls FUN_00435e82. The delegate initializes edit control 0x43e, validates the entered name with FUN_00436863, reports duplicate/invalid-name errors, appends accepted names to the input-name array for 0x438 or output-name array for 0x439, and closes the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure thunk for the Variable Name dialog used to rename an existing input/output variable node in the diagram editor.
Evidence: Handles custom message 0x8008 by storing the current function/model pointer in DAT_0046c4e0, otherwise delegates to FUN_004362c1. DialogBoxParamA(..., "VARNAMEDLG", ..., FUN_0040adf6, selectedIndex) is invoked on double-click of diagram objects whose type is 8 or 9. The delegate initializes edit control 0x43e, validates via FUN_00436863, updates input/output name arrays and the selected diagram object label, then calls EndDialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the gate-label edit dialog GATELABELDLG; stores the active diagram/controller pointer, then delegates dialog handling to FUN_00436748, which edits the selected non-input/output gate object's label text.
Evidence: Main diagram-entry setup calls FUN_0040ae38(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1a4), and the function stores param_4 in DAT_0046c4e4. On double-click in the diagram editor, non input/output objects use DialogBoxParamA(..., "GATELABELDLG", ..., FUN_0040ae38, selectedIndex), while type 8/9 variable nodes use VARNAMEDLG. FUN_00436748 handles WM_INITDIALOG, saves selected object index, limits edit control 0x43e to 0x10 chars, loads current label from selected object offset 0x50, and writes GetDlgItemTextA(..., 0x43e, ..., 0x11) back before EndDialog(1).

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the modeless Trace Gate Logic dialog TRACEDLG16. It binds the active gate-diagram/function object, then delegates dialog behavior to FUN_00436c32, which displays/toggles input values, recomputes traced gate states, and redraws the diagram with trace coloring.
Evidence: Call sites invoke FUN_0040ae7a(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac), then CreateDialogParamA(..., "TRACEDLG16", ..., FUN_0040ae7a, 0). Menu command 0x152 corresponds to Trace Gate Logic; toggling it creates/destroys this dialog and sends diagram commands 0x8013/0x8014. FUN_0040ae7a stores the object pointer in DAT_0046c4e8 on message 0x8008, otherwise calls FUN_00436c32. The delegate initializes up to 16 input controls 0x45d..0x46c, labels them with "%s = %d", toggles truth values, recomputes/redraws, invalidates the diagram window, handles Tracing_logic.htm help, and closes through command 0x152.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the IC Package Info dialog; stores the selected function/model pointer, then delegates dialog initialization/close handling to the model-specific handler that displays precomputed IC package counts.
Evidence: Main command 0x156 opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "ICPKGINFO", ..., FUN_0040aebc, 0) after exactly one function is selected. FUN_0040aebc(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac) stores the active model pointer in DAT_0046c4ec; normal messages delegate to FUN_0042424d, which handles WM_INITDIALOG by setting dialog item 0x43e from this + 0x274 and closes on OK/Cancel. FUN_00423a22 fills that buffer with lines such as IC\\tQty, Hex Inverter, Quad 2-Input NAND, Quad 2-Input MUX, and TOTAL PACKAGES from mapped diagram gate counts.

Inferred responsibility: Print dialog hook/trampoline for Logic Friday's custom PRINTDLG template; forwards print-dialog messages to the global print-controller object so the dialog can choose between diagram versus equation printing and open page setup.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_0041c581(&DAT_00453ce8, hwnd, msg, commandId). FUN_0041a687 installs it into PRINTDLGA as the print hook, sets template name "PRINTDLG", title "Logic Friday: Printing", then calls PrintDlgA. FUN_0041c581 initializes radio buttons 0x425/0x426, disables diagram printing when the selected function has no diagram objects, updates mode field this + 0xc0, and opens PageSetupDlgA through FUN_0041a43b for control 0x424. After PrintDlgA, caller branches to textual equation printing or gate-diagram printing.

Inferred responsibility: Print-cancel modeless dialog procedure trampoline for PRNCNCLDLG; it forwards dialog messages to the print controller and lets the user cancel an active print job.
Evidence: FUN_0041ac36 and FUN_0041b260 create PRNCNCLDLG with CreateDialogParamA(..., FUN_0040af1b, 0) during printing, disable the main window, store the dialog handle at print-controller offset +4, set cancel flag +0xb4 to 0, then call SetAbortProc(..., FUN_0040af38). FUN_0040af1b calls FUN_0041c6a8(&DAT_00453ce8, hwnd, msg). FUN_0041c6a8 sets title "Logic Friday" on init and on command sets cancel flag +0xb4 to 1, re-enables owner, destroys the dialog, and clears the handle.

Inferred responsibility: Win32 printer abort procedure callback for Logic Friday print jobs; pumps the UI message queue during printing and tells GDI whether printing should continue, honoring the print-cancel dialog state.
Evidence: Body is FUN_0041c712(0x453ce8), using global print/controller object DAT_00453ce8. Both print paths call SetAbortProc(HDC, FUN_0040af38) after creating PRNCNCLDLG and before StartDocA. FUN_0041c712 loops PeekMessageA, routes messages through IsDialogMessageA for the cancel dialog, calls TranslateMessage/DispatchMessageA, and returns cancelFlag == 0. The cancel dialog proc FUN_0040af1b -> FUN_0041c6a8 sets that flag to 1.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the NAMESORDERDLG choice dialog used when two functions have the same input variable names but in a different order; lets the caller distinguish cancel, operate by matching variable names, or use raw truth-table input ordering.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_00416425. FUN_00416425 returns TRUE on WM_INITDIALOG, and on WM_COMMAND ends the dialog with result 2, 0x40b, or 0x40c. Call sites pass it to DialogBoxParamA(..., "NAMESORDERDLG", ...) after FUN_00416134 returns 1, meaning all input names exist in both functions but at different indexes. Dialog result 0x40b makes caller pass 1 to FUN_004161ea to remap the second function truth-table rows by matching names; result 0x40c keeps direct truth-table ordering; cancel aborts.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the compare-functions result dialog COMPRESDLG. It delegates to FUN_004164b1, which displays whether two selected one-output functions are equal, subsets, complements, or unequal, plus counts/labels for shared and function-only truth-table cases; command 0x40e closes with nonzero result so the caller can create/export the comparison result function.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_004164b1(&DAT_00452c50, hwnd, msg, command). Caller FUN_00415dd9 is reached from menu command 0xbb after exactly two functions are selected; it validates same input count and one output, reconciles input ordering if needed, compares truth-table values, sets result fields, then opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "COMPRESDLG", ..., FUN_0040af6c, 0). Delegate writes strings such as "%s equals %s.", "%s is a subset of %s.", "%s is the complement of %s.", "%s is not equal to %s.", "%s and %s:", and "%s only:" to controls 0x410..0x417.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the NEWFCNNAME dialog used during function operations to collect the name for a newly created result function. It delegates all behavior to FUN_00416ff2.
Evidence: Only call site found is DialogBoxParamA(..., "NEWFCNNAME", ..., FUN_0040af89, local_20) inside FUN_00416a26 after two selected functions are validated for a function operation. FUN_00416ff2 handles WM_INITDIALOG by storing lParam in DAT_0046c510, limiting edit control 0x418 to 8 chars, initializing the buffer and setting edit text. On OK it reads GetDlgItemTextA(hwnd, 0x418, DAT_0046c510, 9) and EndDialog(..., 1); on cancel it EndDialog(..., 2). Caller copies local_20 into the new function record name field.

Inferred responsibility: Win32 dialog procedure wrapper for LOOKUPFCNDLG, the Generate C Lookup Function options dialog. It delegates to FUN_004170c1, which lets the user choose lookup-table packing width and generated C function call style.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_004170c1(&DAT_00452c50, hwnd, msg, wParam, lParam). Caller opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "LOOKUPFCNDLG", ..., FUN_0040afa6, 0x4519a4) from Generate C Lookup Function flow; before dialog _DAT_004519b0 is set to selected function input count, and after OK the option block DAT_004519a4 is passed to FUN_004133f5, which writes C lookup functions. FUN_004170c1 initializes radio groups 0x419..0x41c and 0x420..0x423, validates/customizes unsigned-int length 8..64, warns for non-byte-aligned custom lengths, updates the option block, and opens Creating_lookup.htm help.

Inferred responsibility: `DiagInWindow` window procedure.
Evidence: Registered under `"DiagInWindow"` by `FUN_0040145f`; used for diagram-entry interaction.

Inferred responsibility: `DiagOutWindow` window procedure.
Evidence: Registered under `"DiagOutWindow"` by `FUN_0040145f`; receives application-defined command messages from the main dispatcher.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the EQNMODVAR modal notice shown after modifying an equation when the input variable order may have changed; it only closes on OK/Cancel and opens help for variable ordering syntax.
Evidence: Used only as DialogBoxParamA(..., "EQNMODVAR", ..., FUN_0040b055, 0). Body returns TRUE on WM_INITDIALOG, closes with EndDialog(..., 1) for OK and EndDialog(..., 2) for Cancel, and on control 0x490 opens %s\\lf.chm::/Syntax.htm#VarOrder via the HTML Help wrapper. Caller shows it when FUN_0040e7da(oldFunction, newFunction) indicates same variables in a different order and the new equation text does not contain INORDER.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the RichEdit logic-equation entry control. It changes the cursor to wait during busy operations and intercepts equation-entry keyboard shortcuts: Enter submits, Ctrl+Enter inserts newline, Escape cancels, and Ctrl+V invokes custom paste; otherwise it forwards to the original RichEdit proc.
Evidence: FUN_00439623 creates DAT_00452ab0 as a RichEdit-backed text control, then DAT_00452ab0 is subclassed with SetWindowLongA(..., 0x40b12d), saving old proc in DAT_00452918. FUN_0040b12d handles WM_SETCURSOR with wait cursor when busy flags are set. WM_KEYDOWN is handled only when DAT_00452e98 is active during equation entry. Enter posts command 0x8000 to parse/set equation, Escape sends command 0x150 cancel path, Ctrl+V posts command 0xae custom paste path, and unhandled messages call CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452918, ...).

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the main function-list ListView; while long-running operations are active it forces the wait cursor on WM_SETCURSOR, otherwise it forwards messages to the original ListView procedure.
Evidence: During main-window initialization, FUN_0040e929 creates DAT_00452ac0 as a SysListView32 with columns such as Function, Inputs, Outputs, False, and Gates; SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ac0, -4, 0x40b2da) installs this function and stores old proc in DAT_00452aa8. Function handles only WM_SETCURSOR: if busy flags DAT_00452e7c, DAT_00452eb0, DAT_00452eb4, DAT_00452ed0, or DAT_00452ed4 are set, it loads IDC_WAIT and returns 1; otherwise it calls CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa8, ...).

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the truth-table output SysListView32; while long-running operations are active it forces the wait cursor on WM_SETCURSOR, otherwise forwards messages to the original list-view window procedure.
Evidence: Startup creates two SysListView32 controls via FUN_00417acd; the first handle is DAT_00452ab4, then subclassed with SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ab4, GWL_WNDPROC, 0x40b34e), storing old proc in DAT_00452aa4. FUN_0040b34e handles WM_SETCURSOR when busy flags are set, calls LoadCursorA(NULL, 0x7f02) / SetCursor, returns 1, and otherwise calls CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa4, ...). Main-window notification handling identifies DAT_00452ab4 as truth-table output by loading context menu MENU_TTOUT when focused.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the truth-table entry ListView; intercepts Escape and routes it to the main window as the truth-table cancel command, otherwise delegates to the original ListView window procedure.
Evidence: Startup FUN_00417acd creates two SysListView32 controls for truth-table UI, then SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ab8, -4, 0x40b3c2) subclasses the second one and stores old proc in DAT_00452aa0. Body checks WM_KEYDOWN and VK_ESCAPE, sends WM_COMMAND 0xe6 to main window DAT_00452aac, and returns 1; all other messages call CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa0, ...). Main command handling treats 0xe6 as the cancel truth-table entry path; resources include truth-table menu items Cancel Esc and Submit Enter.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure that forces the wait cursor over a specific child/control window while long-running Logic Friday operations are active; otherwise forwards messages to the original window procedure.
Evidence: Installed with DAT_00452a9c = SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452a28, GWL_WNDPROC, 0x40b41f). Body handles only WM_SETCURSOR when busy flags DAT_00452e7c, DAT_00452eb0, DAT_00452eb4, DAT_00452ed0, or DAT_00452ed4 are set, loads IDC_WAIT, calls SetCursor, and returns 1. Otherwise delegates with CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452a9c, ...). Same wait-cursor subclass pattern appears in FUN_0040b12d, FUN_0040b2da, and FUN_0040b34e.

Inferred responsibility: Reset and release the temporary truth-table/function-staging structure, freeing each allocated output truth-table array before clearing the whole 0x1f0-byte block.
Evidence: Body loops over count at param+0xc8, frees non-null pointers at param+0x84+index*4, then _memset(param,0,0x1f0). Callers pass &DAT_0046c2d0 before rebuilding input names, output names, truth-table row count, and output arrays for generated/test truth tables.

Inferred responsibility: Allocate or find a free function slot.
Evidence: Called before creating/opening new functions; negative return is treated as allocation failure.

Inferred responsibility: Duplicate the currently selected function into a new function-list slot and initialize the cloned model for use in the UI.
Evidence: Body grows DAT_004528a4 by one 0x118-byte slot, allocates a 0x2700-byte model, calls FUN_0041cbd4, stores the clone at slot+0x110, copies model dirty/state field from model+0x23c to slot+0x114, clears slot name/path strings, calls FUN_0041def5 to bind the model to the main window/user.genlib path, and returns the new slot index. Main command 0xde calls it after validating exactly one selected function.

Inferred responsibility: Free/release a function slot.
Evidence: Called after open/load failure and close/delete flows.

Inferred responsibility: Function-model destructor/free wrapper: run model cleanup and optionally free the model allocation.
Evidence: Body calls FUN_0041d5e1, which FunctionsMapping.md maps as function-model cleanup/destruction, then frees this when param_1 bit 0 is set. FUN_0040b784 calls FUN_0040b86b(this,1) when removing a function slot.

Inferred responsibility: Find a function-list slot by case-insensitive function name.
Evidence: Body scans DAT_004528a4 from index 0 to DAT_004528a0, compares the slot name at slot base with the supplied string using __stricmp, returns the matching index, or -1 when not found. Callers use FUN_0040b894("Z") to locate generated/test function slots.

Inferred responsibility: Resolve function object pointer to slot/index.
Evidence: Used before saving, close prompts, and filename lookup in `DAT_004528a4`.

Inferred responsibility: MINTEST dialog procedure that collects parameters for the testmin minimization test: input count and percent-true denominator, packed into the caller option word.
Evidence: DialogBoxParamA opens resource MINTEST with FUN_0040b947 and lParam 0x45bf04. WM_INITDIALOG stores the option pointer and initializes spinner controls 0x3f1/0x3f3 from DAT_004519cc/DAT_004519c8. On OK it reads edit controls 0x3f0/0x3f2, packs high-word inputs and low-word denominator into *DAT_0046c4f8, and ends with 1. Caller later uses those fields for random truth density in the testmin flow.

Inferred responsibility: EQNTEST dialog procedure that collects equation-test generation settings: input count, output count, checkbox option, mode radio choice, and repetition/count value.
Evidence: DialogBoxParamA opens resource EQNTEST with FUN_0040ba7d and lParam 0x45bf18. WM_INITDIALOG initializes up-down controls 0x484/0x486/0x488, checkbox 0x48d, and radio group 0x42a/0x408/0x42b from DAT_004519dc/DAT_004519d8/DAT_004519d4/DAT_0046c4fc/DAT_004519d0. On OK it writes five values to the option block. Command handler later posts 0x8022 with mode 3 and consumes these settings to generate/check equation test cases.

Inferred responsibility: RANDOMTT dialog procedure that asks for the random truth-table percent/density value and returns it as the modal dialog result.
Evidence: DialogBoxParamA opens resource RANDOMTT with FUN_0040bd02. WM_INITDIALOG initializes spinner 0x475 with range 1..100 and current DAT_004519e0. On OK it reads edit control 0x481 into DAT_004519e0 and EndDialog returns that value; on cancel it returns -1. Caller command 0xe7 treats the result as random truth-table density.

Inferred responsibility: Set status-bar pane 0 text, used for primary application status messages such as Ready, Working, Parsing, Minimizing, Mapping, and Setting equation.
Evidence: DAT_00452abc is created with CreateStatusWindowA(...,"Ready",...). FUN_0040bdc3 checks that handle and sends status-bar message 0x401 with wParam 0 and lParam text pointer. Call sites pass status strings such as Ready, Working, Minimizing, and Mapping.

Inferred responsibility: Set status-bar pane 1 text, used for secondary progress/counter messages during generated test loops and long operations.
Evidence: DAT_00452abc is the status window. FUN_0040bde7 checks that handle and sends message 0x401 with wParam 1 and lParam text pointer. Call sites format progress strings such as Count = %d and %d%%: %d before passing them to FUN_0040bde7.

Inferred responsibility: Generate and reserve the next unique auto-name from the DAT_0044a700 short name format by advancing the global DAT_0046c504 counter until no function-list entry already uses that name.
Evidence: Body repeatedly formats local_2c from DAT_0044a700, scans all function-list slots with __stricmp against DAT_004528a4 slot names, increments DAT_0046c504 on collision, and returns the first unused counter value. FUN_00416ff2 calls it before filling NEWFCNNAME; truth-table setup/generation paths also use the same auto-name format.

Inferred responsibility: Computes the numeric suffix needed to make a generated function name unique against the global function list; returns 0 when the proposed name is unused, 2 when only the unsuffixed name collides, or max existing suffix + 1 for matching generated names.
Evidence: Body formats a fixed generated-name prefix into local_24, scans DAT_004528a4 function records using __strnicmp against that prefix, parses trailing number with _atol, tracks maximum, and if no numbered match exists compares each existing function name to param_1 with __stricmp. Callers use the nonzero result before inserting generated function names.

Inferred responsibility: About dialog procedure.
Evidence: Dialog resource `"ABOUTDLG"` uses this proc; handles hyperlink clicks through `FUN_004013e5`.

Inferred responsibility: Complete or refresh output after external minimization/mapping finishes.
Evidence: Sets busy flag, updates output view via `FUN_0043983d`, changes status text, calls cleanup/status helpers.

Inferred responsibility: Run Espresso or MISII asynchronously with redirected output and cancellation support.
Evidence: Builds `%s\\espresso\\espresso.exe` or `%s\\misii\\misii.exe`, creates `%s\\minout.dat`, starts process with inherited stdout/stderr, waits on both cancel event and process, posts completion message `0x8004`/`0x8006`.

Inferred responsibility: Run Espresso or MISII synchronously, redirecting output to `minout.dat`.
Evidence: Same executable selection as `FUN_0040c196`, calls `CreateProcessA`, waits up to 3,600,000 ms, sends `WM_COMMAND 0x8004`/`0x8006`.

Inferred responsibility: Run MISII synchronously for `checkout.dat` generation.
Evidence: Uses `%s\\misii\\misii.exe`, redirects stdout/stderr to `%s\\checkout.dat`, sends `WM_COMMAND 0x8021`.

Inferred responsibility: Check or uncheck a menu item.
Evidence: Thin wrapper over `CheckMenuItem`.

Inferred responsibility: Enable or disable a menu item.
Evidence: Thin wrapper over `EnableMenuItem`.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for NEWFCNDLG; lets the user choose what kind of new function to create: truth table, logic equation, or gate diagram, with cancel returning -1.
Evidence: Returns TRUE on WM_INITDIALOG, handles WM_COMMAND IDCANCEL by EndDialog(...,-1), control 0x425 by EndDialog(...,2), control 0x426 by EndDialog(...,1), and control 0x428 by EndDialog(...,0). Caller opens NEWFCNDLG for command 0x81 and maps return 0 to command 0x83, return 1 to 0x82, and return 2 to 0xcd.

Inferred responsibility: Modeless GATEPICKDLG2 dialog procedure for the gate-diagram palette; positions the palette beside the main window, loads button icons, forwards palette command clicks to the active diagram-entry window, and closes/cancels diagram entry on OK/Cancel.
Evidence: WM_INITDIALOG positions the dialog relative to DAT_00452aac and loads image resources into controls 0x42c and 0x42b. WM_COMMAND posts Escape to DAT_00452a98 for OK/Cancel, opens help via main command 0x801b for 0x428, and forwards gate/wire/palette commands such as 0x42b and 0x42c to DAT_00452a98. Created as GATEPICKDLG2 when entering gate-diagram mode.

Inferred responsibility: Create and populate the toolbar.
Evidence: Creates `ToolbarWindow32`, assigns image lists, adds buttons via toolbar messages.

Inferred responsibility: Refreshes enabled/disabled state of the main toolbar buttons according to current selection, edit mode, busy operations, diagram availability, and active input/output panes.
Evidence: Called after resize, selection changes, command completion, and diagram updates. It computes busy/edit flags from DAT_00452e7c/DAT_00452eb0/DAT_00452ed8 and DAT_00452e98/DAT_00452e90/DAT_00452e94, queries selected function with FUN_0040f186, checks diagram state via command 0x8016, queries RichEdit state with FUN_00439b85, then calls FUN_0040d2b9 for many toolbar command IDs.

Inferred responsibility: Enables or disables one toolbar button by command ID.
Evidence: Body sends TB_ENABLEBUTTON (0x411) to toolbar handle DAT_00452a24 with lParam 0x10 when param_2 is 0 and lParam 4 when param_2 is 1. Used by FUN_0040cf1c while refreshing toolbar state.

Inferred responsibility: Looks up toolbar tooltip/help text for a toolbar command ID.
Evidence: Scans toolbar definition table entries, compares param_1 to command ID field, and returns the text field. Main WM_NOTIFY handling calls it for tooltip notification code 0xfffffdf8, then copies returned string into the notification text buffer with lstrcpyA.

Inferred responsibility: Builds and displays the appropriate right-click context menu for the current focused Logic Friday pane, with menu items enabled/checked according to selection, edit mode, busy state, and diagram/truth-table state.
Evidence: Called from main WM_CONTEXTMENU handler with screen coordinates. It verifies click is inside client area, checks edit/busy flags, determines focus, loads context menus MENU_TTOUT, MENU_LEOUT, MENU_DIAGOUT, MENU_TTIN, MENU_LEIN, or MENU_DIAGIN, enables items through FUN_0040ca01, checks Trace Gate Logic when needed, calls TrackPopupMenu and DestroyMenu, and queries selected/diagram/input state.

Inferred responsibility: Normalizes an equation/input text buffer by replacing carriage-return characters with spaces.
Evidence: Body loops over strlen(param_1) and changes each '\r' to ' '. Called after equation text passes FUN_0040dc71 validation and before multi-output equation parsing through FUN_00413fc6.

Inferred responsibility: Validates a variable/function name string for characters that are illegal in Logic Friday names; returns 0 if valid or packed error code 0x1f00XX for the first forbidden character found.
Evidence: Searches for forbidden characters @, #, double quote, backslash, {, }, &, *, |, +, !, ^, =, and comma, returning packed codes such as 0x1f0040 or 0x1f002c. Used while parsing CSV headers and validating variable/function names.

Inferred responsibility: Prevalidates logic-equation text for characters that are illegal in equation input/import text while allowing normal logic operators; returns 0 if valid or packed error code 0x1f00XX for the offending character.
Evidence: Checks only @, #, double quote, backslash, {, }, and comma, unlike FUN_0040daf0 which also rejects logic operators. Called before equation import/entry parsing; successful validation is followed by parsing/minimization setup, failures pass through packed error-code handling.

Inferred responsibility: Heuristic predicate for whether gate mapping may be slow enough to show the MAPTIME warning dialog.
Evidence: Called only in Map to Gates command path before MAPTIME dialog and only when WarnMapTime is enabled. Examines selected function model input count, output count, and per-output term counts, returning 1 when counts fall into ranges likely to make mapping expensive.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the TTEXPORTWARN warning; OK/Cancel close the dialog and the checkbox disables future truth-table export warnings.
Evidence: Used as DialogBoxParamA(...,TTEXPORTWARN...,FUN_0040e0b2,0) before exporting multi-output truth tables when WarnTTExport is enabled. Handles WM_INITDIALOG, OK, and Cancel; if checkbox 0x48d is checked it clears DAT_00452ee8, then ends with result 1 or 2.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the MAPTIME warning; OK/Cancel close the dialog and the checkbox disables future gate-mapping time warnings.
Evidence: Used as DialogBoxParamA(...,MAPTIME...,FUN_0040e14c,0) when FUN_0040dd2e predicts expensive gate mapping and WarnMapTime is enabled. Handles WM_INITDIALOG, OK, and Cancel; if checkbox 0x48d is checked it clears DAT_00452ee0, then returns result 1 or 2.

Inferred responsibility: Heuristic predicate for whether Espresso minimization may be slow enough to show the MINTIMEDLG warning dialog.
Evidence: Called in Minimize command path before MINTIMEDLG and only when WarnMinTime is enabled. Checks function input count, output count, and per-output term counts against thresholds such as input counts 0xb..0x10 and term-count cutoffs 0x266, 0x4cc, 0x998, and 0x1330.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the MINTIMEDLG warning; OK/Cancel close the dialog and the checkbox disables future minimization time warnings.
Evidence: Used as DialogBoxParamA(...,MINTIMEDLG...,FUN_0040e3cf,0) when FUN_0040e1e6 predicts expensive minimization and WarnMinTime is enabled. Handles WM_INITDIALOG, OK, and Cancel; if checkbox 0x48d is checked it clears DAT_00452ee4, then ends with result 1 or 2.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the MINDLG minimization options dialog; maps selected radio-button combination to one of four Espresso command modes.
Evidence: Minimize command opens MINDLG and maps return values 0x3e9, 0x3ea, 0x3eb, and 0x3ec to Espresso commands -Dso, default, -Dso -S1, and -Dexact. Dialog initializes radio groups 0x408/0x409 and 0x42b/0x42c, disables multi-output option group when output count is 1, persists selections on OK, and returns 2 on cancel.

Inferred responsibility: Prepare or validate a file operation caption/path context.
Evidence: Called before `"File Open"` and other file workflows; exact UI side effect not fully mapped.

Inferred responsibility: Refreshes wait-cursor state on the main work panes by posting WM_SETCURSOR to each subclassed child window.
Evidence: Body posts message 0x20 to DAT_00452ac0, DAT_00452ab0, DAT_00452ab4, and DAT_00452a2c. Those handles are the function-list ListView, equation RichEdit, truth-table output ListView, and diagram-output window; their subclass procedures use busy flags to force IDC_WAIT during long operations.

Inferred responsibility: Detects whether a modified equation changed input-variable order in a way that should trigger the EQNMODVAR variable-order warning.
Evidence: Called after modifying a function; caller opens EQNMODVAR when this function returns 0. Returns 1 when input counts differ, new equation text contains INORDER, names are already in same order, or names are not just a permutation; returns 0 when both functions contain the same input names in a different order.

Inferred responsibility: Initializes the function-list controller object's basic state before the ListView is created.
Evidence: Called from static initializer with &DAT_00452ad0. Body sets controller offsets +0x50 to 0, +0x54 to 0, and +0x58 to 1, matching initial item count/selection/default state later used by the function-list controller.

Inferred responsibility: Initialize paths, temp/work files, file dialogs, and verify bundled tools.
Evidence: Uses `GetModuleFileNameA`, `SHGetSpecialFolderPathA`, `CreateDirectoryA`, sets `lf.ini`, `minin.dat`, `minout.dat`, `checkin.dat`, `checkout.dat`, `user.genlib`, checks `espresso.exe`, `misii.exe`, and `script.txt` with `SearchPathA`.

Inferred responsibility: Add/register a function item in the list/controller.
Evidence: Called after open/import/create; argument points to per-function slot data.

Inferred responsibility: Get current selected function from a list/controller.
Evidence: Many command handlers call it and branch on zero, one, or multiple selected functions.

Inferred responsibility: Get two selected functions.
Evidence: Used for two-function operations; returns count and output pointers.

Inferred responsibility: Select/focus a function by index in the list/controller.
Evidence: Called before save/close and after creating/opening functions.

Inferred responsibility: Updates an existing row in the main function-list ListView for a given function model, including counts/status columns and selection/scroll state.
Evidence: Searches ListView items for row whose stored lParam/function pointer equals param_1, sends LVM_SETITEM-style messages for columns Function, Inputs, Outputs, False, Gates, writes Unminimized when model is not minimized, Not mapped when no diagram exists, otherwise counts mapped gates from diagram-object array, clears prior selected states, selects updated row, ensures visibility, and scrolls it into view.

Inferred responsibility: Remove a function from the list/controller.
Evidence: Used during close/delete flows before freeing the backing function.

Inferred responsibility: Function-list notification handler that guards selection changes: if selection is cleared it restores the last selected function, and if exactly one real function is selected it signals the main window to confirm/update active function selection.
Evidence: Called from main WM_NOTIFY when hwnd is DAT_00452ac0, the main function-list ListView. It checks LVM_GETSELECTEDCOUNT, calls FUN_0040f300 when selection count is 0 and list has real items, and writes -2 to out parameter when selected count is 1. Caller treats -2 specially, prompts with Function Selection, then sends command 0x801d.

Inferred responsibility: Return function count.
Evidence: Used after closing functions to decide if any remain.

Inferred responsibility: Reports whether the function-list controller has moved past its initial placeholder state and contains real selectable functions.
Evidence: Returns *(param_1 + 0x58) == 0. FUN_0040e905 initializes +0x58 to 1; FUN_0040eb81 removes placeholder on first real function insert and sets +0x58 to 0. Caller uses this after FUN_0040fb46 reports one-selection event.

Inferred responsibility: Constructor/initializer for the file/environment helper object used for Logic Friday paths, temporary files, and external-tool workflows.
Evidence: Static initializer calls FUN_0040fbf0(&DAT_00452f40) and registers cleanup. Body initializes version string v. 1.0 and empty path/name buffers, then clears counters/flags. Same global is later passed to open/save/export helpers and startup path/tool validation.

Inferred responsibility: Destructor/cleanup for the file/environment helper object; releases the cached resource pointer stored at offset +0x268 when present.
Evidence: Registered by static initializer path after constructing DAT_00452f40. Body only checks *(param_1 + 0x268) and calls DeleteObject on it if non-null.

Inferred responsibility: Startup initialization for Logic Friday file/environment helper: establishes application/user temp directories, prepares paths for lf.ini/minin.dat/minout.dat/checkin.dat/checkout.dat/user.genlib, initializes OPENFILENAME state, and verifies bundled Espresso/MISII files exist.
Evidence: Called during main-window creation. Uses GetModuleFileNameA and FUN_00415864 to derive install path, SHGetSpecialFolderPathA/CreateDirectoryA for app data Logic Friday directory or fallback temp directory, formats paths for lf.ini, minout.dat, minin.dat, checkout.dat, checkin.dat, and user.genlib, initializes OPENFILENAME-like fields, then SearchPathA-checks espresso.exe, misii.exe, and misii\lib\script.txt; shows fatal file error on failure.

Inferred responsibility: Show Open dialog for Logic function files.
Evidence: Filter string `"Logic function (*.lfcn)"`, uses `GetOpenFileNameA`, stores selected open directory.

Inferred responsibility: Show Save dialog for Logic function files.
Evidence: Filter string `"Logic function (*.lfcn)"`, uses `GetSaveFileNameA`, stores selected save directory.

Inferred responsibility: Show Save dialog for C source export.
Evidence: Filter string `"C Source File (*.c)"`, uses `GetSaveFileNameA`.

Inferred responsibility: Show Save dialog for enhanced metafile export.
Evidence: Filter string `"Enhanced Metafile (*.emf)"`, uses `GetSaveFileNameA`.

Inferred responsibility: Show Open dialog for arbitrary files.
Evidence: Filter string `"All Files (*.*)"`, uses `GetOpenFileNameA`.

Inferred responsibility: Show Save dialog for CSV truth-table export.
Evidence: Filter string `"Comma Separated Variables (*.csv)"`, uses `GetSaveFileNameA`.

Inferred responsibility: Export gate diagram image to `.emf` or `.bmp`.
Evidence: For `.emf`, calls `CopyEnhMetaFileA`; otherwise renders the EMF to a 1-bit DIB and writes BMP headers/data via `_fwrite`.

Inferred responsibility: Export truth table to CSV/text.
Evidence: Opens output as `wt`, writes input/output names and rows, emits `0`, `1`, and `X` values, supports both full truth table and reduced/lookup table representation.

Inferred responsibility: Import CSV/text truth table.
Evidence: Opens input as `rt`, skips comments/blank lines, parses names and rows, validates input/output counts, allocates output arrays, fills truth table.

Inferred responsibility: Parse one truth-table data row into input and output token strings.
Evidence: Splits on comma/tab, accepts only `0`, `1`, `X`/`x`, normalizes lowercase `x`.

Inferred responsibility: Parse and validate CSV variable-name header row.
Evidence: Splits input/output names, enforces length limits, validates characters through `FUN_0040daf0`, rejects duplicate input/output names.

Inferred responsibility: Apply one parsed truth-table row to the in-memory table, expanding input don't-cares.
Evidence: Converts input bits to row index; if input contains `X`, enumerates all matching rows; delegates cell assignment to `FUN_00411dfc`.

Inferred responsibility: Assign output values for one truth-table row and detect conflicts.
Evidence: Writes `1`, `0`, or `2` (`X`) into output arrays; increments true/don't-care counts; returns conflict code `10` if row already has different value.

Inferred responsibility: Show import error dialogs.
Evidence: Maps import error codes 1-10 to `"Import Error"` message boxes.

Inferred responsibility: Save a Logic Friday `.lfcn` function file.
Evidence: Opens output as `wb`, writes `"LTK1"`-style header/version data, fixed 0x2700-byte function state, truth-table arrays, text fields, mapping/gate data, optional EMF bits, then marks function clean.

Inferred responsibility: Load a Logic Friday `.lfcn` function file.
Evidence: Opens input as `rb`, verifies `"LTK1"`, checks stored version against `DAT_004519e4`, reads 0x2700-byte base state, allocates truth-table and mapping buffers, restores optional EMF via `SetEnhMetaFileBits`.

Inferred responsibility: Destructor/deallocator for a vector of 0xfc-byte objects.
Evidence: Calls `_eh_vector_destructor_iterator_` and `_free` depending on flags.

Inferred responsibility: Generates a C lookup-table source file for the selected function, optionally including a test wrapper, then opens the generated file in Notepad.
Evidence: Reached from Generate C Lookup Function after LOOKUPFCNDLG succeeds and save dialog chooses a lookup output file. Opens param_3 for text write, emits Logic Friday timestamp comment, writes prototypes/implementations named lu_%s, serializes output truth tables into const unsigned int arrays, supports unsigned int nTerm or individual input arguments, optionally calls FUN_00413c2e when debug tools are enabled, closes file, builds notepad command, and calls WinExec.

Inferred responsibility: Emits the optional TestLUFcnZ C helper for generated lookup functions, converting a row index into input arguments when the generated lookup API uses individual variables, then delegating output dispatch generation.
Evidence: Only called by FUN_004133f5 when DAT_00452ef4 is enabled. Writes int TestLUFcnZ(int iRow,int iOut). If option is 0x423 it uses iRow directly; otherwise declares each input variable, emits bit tests from iRow, builds comma-separated argument list from input names, and calls FUN_00413e13.

Inferred responsibility: Writes the switch body for TestLUFcnZ, dispatching iOut values to lu_F0 through lu_F15 and returning 0 by default.
Evidence: Called from FUN_00413c2e with argument expression and output count. Body writes switch(iOut), always emits case 0 returning lu_F0(%s), conditionally emits cases 1..15 depending on output count, then writes closing default return 0 block.

Inferred responsibility: Writes the current equation text to minin.dat as input for the MISII debug/minimization path.
Evidence: In command path that validates typed equation text then starts FUN_0040c196 with misii re/wp command, this function is called first. Body deletes file at this +0x784, initialized as %s\minin.dat, opens it for text write, writes param_1, closes it, and returns 0 or packed file error 0x2f000b.

Inferred responsibility: Parses MISII/Espresso PLA output from minout.dat back into a Logic Friday function model, including input/output counts, variable names, product count, and truth-table rows.
Evidence: Called after external MISII run completes in minout.dat path. Opens this +0x888 (%s\minout.dat), expects PLA directives .i, .o, .ilb, .ob, .p, enforces 2..16 inputs and at most 16 outputs, copies names, allocates table data via FUN_00421c38, loads product rows with FUN_00421d2a, finalizes via FUN_00421eb4, and returns parse/file errors when needed.

Inferred responsibility: Write prepared equation text to checkin.dat so MISII can convert it to PLA format.
Evidence: Body opens path at this +0x98c, initialized as %s\checkin.dat. It writes param_1 to the file and returns 0x2b0001 on open failure. Caller command 0x8020 builds INORDER/OUTORDER plus selected equation text, calls this function, then runs misii read_eqn checkin.dat/write_pla.

Inferred responsibility: Parse MISII checkout.dat PLA output back into a function/truth-table record after equation-to-PLA conversion.
Evidence: Opens this +0xa90 (%s\checkout.dat), validates .i, .o, .ilb, .ob, .p records, stores input/output counts and names, allocates product rows with FUN_00421c38, reads each PLA row via FUN_00421d2a, finalizes with FUN_00421eb4, and maps MISII error text such as contains a cycle or bad character to Logic Friday error results/messages.

Inferred responsibility: Write external-tool input files (`minin.dat`/`checkin.dat`) before running Espresso/MISII.
Evidence: Called immediately before setting command lines and launching `FUN_0040c196`; surrounding object path fields include `minin.dat`, `checkin.dat`.

Inferred responsibility: Parse minimized PLA/product output from minout.dat into the target function model after an external minimization run completes.
Evidence: Opens this +0x888 (%s\minout.dat), scans PLA header lines until .p, allocates product terms with FUN_00421c38(*(param_1 + 4), count), then reads rows until .e via FUN_00421d2a. Caller after command 0x8004 completion invokes it before refreshing truth-table/function views and creating/updating minimized function.

Inferred responsibility: Copy current gate diagram to the clipboard as enhanced metafile and bitmap.
Evidence: Calls `CopyEnhMetaFileA`, `OpenClipboard`, `EmptyClipboard`, `SetClipboardData(CF_ENHMETAFILE)`, then renders bitmap with `PlayEnhMetaFile`.

Inferred responsibility: Copy another generated metafile view to the clipboard as enhanced metafile and optional bitmap.
Evidence: Creates EMF via `FUN_00436925`, renders bitmap from `GetEnhMetaFileHeader`, writes both clipboard formats.

Inferred responsibility: Load persisted window placement, folder paths, and warning flags from `lf.ini`.
Evidence: Uses `GetPrivateProfileStructA`, `GetPrivateProfileStringA`, and `GetPrivateProfileIntA`.

Inferred responsibility: Save window placement, folder paths, and warning flags to `lf.ini`.
Evidence: Uses `WritePrivateProfileStructA` and `WritePrivateProfileStringA`.

Inferred responsibility: Extract application directory from module path.
Evidence: Finds final backslash; copies directory prefix or defaults to empty string.

Inferred responsibility: Relax security on generated temp files when using the temporary working directory.
Evidence: Creates security descriptor and calls `SetFileSecurityA` for `minin.dat`, `minout.dat`, and `user.genlib`.

Inferred responsibility: Custom destructive tokenizer similar to strtok, using a caller-supplied delimiter-character set and caller-owned cursor pointer.
Evidence: Takes current cursor pointer and delimiter-character set. Returns token start, replaces matched delimiter with NUL, advances cursor, and returns null when cursor is null. Lower-level parsers FUN_004116b2 and FUN_00411871 use it to split comma/tab-separated PLA/import fields.

Inferred responsibility: Trivial constructor/initializer stub that returns the object pointer unchanged.
Evidence: Body is only return param_1. Called from static initialization wrappers for DAT_00452e70 and DAT_00453e28, followed by atexit registration. No fields are modified here.

Inferred responsibility: Apply predefined application UI/workflow state transitions to the global command-state block, controlling modes such as idle, equation entry, truth-table entry, diagram entry, generated/dirty-result state, and long-running follow-up phases.
Evidence: Called throughout main command handling as FUN_00415a02(&DAT_00452e70, stateId) after open/import/compare/minimize/diagram actions. Body switches on state IDs and sets many adjacent globals later read by FUN_0040cf1c and window procs to enable/disable commands, drive edit modes, and show wait cursors.

Inferred responsibility: Initialize the compare-functions controller by clearing its remapped second-function truth-table buffer pointer.
Evidence: Body sets *(param_1 + 0x94) = 0 and returns object pointer. Static initializer calls FUN_00415d83(0x452c50) and registers cleanup. Offset 0x94 is later allocated by FUN_004161ea as copied/remapped truth table for the second selected function.

Inferred responsibility: Destructor/cleanup for the compare-functions controller allocated remapped truth-table buffer.
Evidence: Body frees *(param_1 + 0x94) when non-null. Registered by static initializer through atexit. FUN_004161ea allocates this same offset for comparison data.

Inferred responsibility: Bind the compare-functions controller to the main window and application instance handles.
Evidence: Body stores param_2 at offset 0 and param_1 at offset 4. Startup calls it after creating main UI controls. Later compare dialog code uses offset 0 as owner HWND for MessageBox/DialogBoxParam and offset 4 as HINSTANCE.

Inferred responsibility: Run compare-functions workflow for two selected one-output functions: validate compatibility, reconcile input ordering, compare truth tables, classify equality/subset/complement/inequality, show comparison result dialog, and flag whether to create/export a result function.
Evidence: Caller menu command 0xbb selects exactly two functions and passes them here. Body validates same input count and single output via FUN_004160ec, handles different/same-but-reordered names via MessageBox or NAMESORDERDLG, remaps second function via FUN_004161ea, counts shared/complement/function-only rows, assigns result kind, opens COMPRESDLG through FUN_0040af6c, and sets output flag when dialog requests result creation.

Inferred responsibility: Validate that two selected functions are compatible for comparison or binary operations: both must have exactly one output and the same input/truth-table size.
Evidence: Called at start of FUN_00415dd9 and FUN_00416a26 before compare/operation work; failure shows message that the two functions must have same number of inputs and only one output. Checks both selected function records at offsets +0xc8 equal 1 and +0xc4 are equal.

Inferred responsibility: Compare input variable names of two selected functions and classify relationship as same order, same set different order, or different names.
Evidence: Iterates input names at each function record +0x160 in 9-byte slots using strcmp. Returns 0 when all names match in same positions, 1 when matching names exist at different indexes, and 2 when a name is absent. Callers use 1 to open NAMESORDERDLG and 2 to ask whether to use raw truth-table ordering.

Inferred responsibility: Build an aligned working copy of the second function truth table, optionally remapping rows so its inputs match the first function variable-name order.
Evidence: Frees prior buffers at this+0x94, copies second function metadata into this+0x10, allocates a truth-table buffer, and either copies the second output table directly or computes bit-position deltas by matching input names before copying rows from reordered indexes. Used by compare and OR/AND/XOR flows after input-name reconciliation.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for NAMESORDERDLG; returns user choice for cancel, match-by-name ordering, or raw truth-table ordering.
Evidence: Handles WM_INITDIALOG by returning TRUE. On WM_COMMAND it EndDialogs with 2 for cancel, 0x40b, or 0x40c. Callers interpret 0x40b as remap by variable names and 0x40c as use truth-table order.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog handler for COMPRESDLG that displays compare-results text and closes either without creating a result or with a create/export-result request.
Evidence: FUN_0040af6c delegates to it. On WM_INITDIALOG it selects messages such as %s equals %s, subset, complement, or not equal based on result fields, fills controls 0x410..0x417, and on WM_COMMAND returns EndDialog 0 for OK or 1 for command 0x40e.

Inferred responsibility: Populate a new two-output comparison-result function record from two selected functions after the compare dialog requests result creation.
Evidence: Called after FUN_00415dd9/COMPRESDLG sets create-result flag, immediately before FUN_0041e0c1 imports DAT_0046c2d0 as a new function. Frees/reallocates output truth-table arrays, copies first function truth table and aligned second table from this+0x94, copies input metadata/names, sets output count to 2, and marks raw-order comparisons with generated input names when needed.

Inferred responsibility: Perform a binary operation between two selected one-output functions and populate a new result function record for OR, AND, or XOR.
Evidence: Main dispatcher calls it for menu IDs 0xbc, 0xbd, and 0xc0 matching OR, AND, XOR Functions. It validates compatibility, reconciles input-name ordering, opens NEWFCNNAME, allocates one output truth table in destination record, then combines row values using OR, AND, or XOR/invert logic while preserving don't-care value 2 and counting ones/don't-cares.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for NEWFCNNAME; initializes and returns the user-entered name for a new function-operation result.
Evidence: FUN_0040af89 delegates to it. On WM_INITDIALOG it stores lParam buffer in DAT_0046c510, limits edit control 0x418 to 8 characters, initializes it, and sets edit text. On OK it reads up to 8 chars into buffer and ends with 1; cancel ends with 2.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog handler for LOOKUPFCNDLG, the Generate C Lookup Function options dialog; collects lookup packing width and generated C call style options.
Evidence: FUN_0040afa6 delegates to it and main dispatcher opens LOOKUPFCNDLG before calling FUN_004133f5. Initializes radio groups from option block, enables custom length edit only for custom option, validates 8/16/custom bit lengths against input count via FUN_004175ae, warns for non-byte-aligned custom lengths, stores selected options, and routes help to Creating_lookup.htm.

Inferred responsibility: Validate that selected unsigned-int lookup packing width can represent all input variables.
Evidence: Called by FUN_004170c1 for 8-bit, 16-bit, and custom lengths. If input count exceeds selected bit length it shows Insufficient Length message and returns false; otherwise true.

Inferred responsibility: Constructor/reset initializer for a 0xfc-byte gate-diagram object/node record; clears label/name fields, value/state fields, connection arrays, rectangle, and sentinel indexes.
Evidence: Body writes empty strings to offsets +0x50 and +0x04, clears state fields, zeroes +0x6c..+0x8b, calls SetRect at +0xc8, sets source/input sentinel indexes such as +0x3c/+0x40/+0xf4/+0xf8/+0xe0 and arrays +0x1c/+0xe4 to -3. Used as vector constructor for arrays of diagram objects.

Inferred responsibility: Resets a gate-diagram object computed trace/value state before reevaluating logic; constants get fixed values and other nodes are marked unknown/uncomputed.
Evidence: If object type is 10 it sets offset +0x14 to 1; if type is 11 it sets +0x14 to 0; otherwise sets +0x14 and four per-input cached values at +0x2c..+0x38 to -3. Callers invoke it while traversing diagram arrays before recomputing values.

Inferred responsibility: Recursively evaluates a gate-diagram object for trace/simulation, computing boolean output from connected source objects and cached input values.
Evidence: Recurses through source indexes at +0x1c when cached value +0x14 is -3, handles constants -2/-1 as 0/1, then computes output by object type: output/pass-through, NOT, NAND/NOR-like reductions, AND/OR-like reductions, XOR, and 3-input mux-style logic. Returns -3 when dependency cannot be evaluated.

Inferred responsibility: Static/default initializer for the truth-table ListView controller state used by DAT_00452b30.
Evidence: Called from global startup/atexit registration with DAT_00452b30. Sets default counts/options at offsets +0xdc/+0xe0..+0xec and clears +0x94/+0x98, matching fields later used by TTSETUP handling for default input/output counts and list-edit state.

Inferred responsibility: Creates and initializes the two truth-table SysListView32 controls, returning their window handles to main window setup.
Evidence: Main initialization calls FUN_00417acd(&DAT_00452b30, hwnd, &DAT_00452ab4, &DAT_00452ab8). Body creates two SysListView32 child windows with IDs 0x66 and 0x65, stores them at controller offsets +0x10/+0x14, returns them through out params, inserts initial columns, enables extended list-view styles, and initializes row/column counters.

Inferred responsibility: Clears the truth-table output/display ListView and frees its backing row cache.
Evidence: Sends delete-all-items to handle offset +0x10, deletes existing columns according to counter +0x6c, resets +0x6c to 1, frees backing allocation at +0x7c, clears it, and zeroes related state. Called before repopulating truth-table display with FUN_00417d61.

Inferred responsibility: Clears the truth-table entry/edit ListView and resets its formatting/state cache.
Evidence: Sends delete-all-items and set-item-count 0 to handle offset +0x14, deletes columns based on +0x68, resets +0x68 to 1, and zeroes state block at +0x9c. FUN_004195f3 calls it before rebuilding truth-table entry grid.

Inferred responsibility: Populates the truth-table output/display ListView from a selected function model, including input columns, output columns, row cache, item count, and all-true/all-false compression flags.
Evidence: Calls FUN_00417c56, builds/reallocates row cache +0x7c from function truth-table arrays and counts, detects all-zero/all-one output cases, optionally compresses sparse displays, adds input-variable columns from +0x160, separator column, output columns from +0xd0, autosizes columns, sets ListView item count, and stores display flags.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure implementation for TTSETUP truth-table setup dialog; manages input/output count spinners, editable variable-name ListViews, validation, allocation of truth-table output arrays, and OK/Cancel/help handling.
Evidence: FUN_0040ad29 delegates TTSETUP messages here. On WM_INITDIALOG it initializes up-down controls, ListViews, default variable names, and existing names during modify mode. On LVN_ENDLABELEDIT it calls FUN_004194bc and reports validation errors. On OK it copies labels, sets counts, computes term count as 1 << inputCount, reallocates output arrays, preserves existing output data when modifying, and ends dialog.

Inferred responsibility: Validates a truth-table setup variable name for length, uniqueness across input/output lists, and legal identifier syntax.
Evidence: Called by FUN_00418362 during ListView label-edit completion. Rejects empty or longer-than-8 names with return 1, scans both input and output ListViews using LVM_GETITEMTEXT and returns 2 for duplicates, then delegates syntax validation to FUN_0040daf0. Caller displays nearby variable-name error messages.

Inferred responsibility: Initializes/populates the truth-table entry ListView after TTSETUP, creating input/output columns, separator columns, row count, and per-output edit lock flags.
Evidence: Called after TTSETUP succeeds. Clears ListView via FUN_00417cde, inserts columns into handle this+0x14, uses setup/model input names at +0x160 and output names at +0xd0, sets column count at this+0x68, zeroes flags at this+0x9c, sends LVM_SETITEMCOUNT from *param_1, and auto-sizes columns.
